cases.title: Sheppard v. Highlands Presbytery
precedent_refs: SJC 2016-05, Troxell v. Presbytery of the Southwest
Lee v. Korean Eastern Presbytery (Case No. 2010-26)
Lyons v. Western Carolina, M39GA 2011, page 594
Ganzel v. Central Florida Presbytery, M47GA 2021, page 729 (Case No 2019-08)
cases.synopsis: TE Craig Sheppard complained that Highlands Presbytery erred by conflating judicial and pastoral procedures after finding a strong presumption of guilt, and by failing to institute process within one year as required by BCO 32-20. The SJC sustained both issues, holding that BCO 31-2 mandates process upon finding a strong presumption of guilt, and that Presbytery was debarred from prosecution because the alleged offense occurred in 2017 and timely process was never instituted; three members dissented on the BCO 32-20 issue, arguing it was not a true statute of limitations and that the matter was never a 'case of scandal.'

cases.title: Crouse et al. v. Northwest Georgia Presbytery
cases.synopsis: The SJC found this complaint against Northwest Georgia Presbytery out of order and moot because the Session had dismissed all charges and the ruling elders who were subjects of process had voluntarily resigned and had their calls dissolved before the SJC acted. The complaint was accordingly dismissed unanimously.

cases.title: Biese et al. v. Tennessee Valley Presbytery
cases.synopsis: The Complainants withdrew this Complaint on July 18, 2022, and the SJC noted such on October 20, 2022. No substantive decision was issued.

cases.title: Harrell et al. v. Covenant Presbytery

parties.complainant_or_appellant: Mr. Paul Harrell et al. (seven members of Christ Redeemer PCA)

precedent_refs: Case 2021-07, RE Acree v. Tennessee Valley (March 2022, Report to 49th GA, p. 2144)
Case 2020-13, Benyola v. Central Florida (M48GA, 2021, p. 817)
Case 2020-01, Benyola v. Central Florida (M48GA, 2021, p. 801)
Case 2012-08, RE Warren Jackson v. NW Georgia (M43GA, 2015, p. 568)
Case 2012-06, Deacon Don Bethel v. SE Alabama (M41GA, 2013, p. 614)
Case 92-9b, Overman v. Eastern Carolina (M21GA, 1993, p. 223)
cases.synopsis: Seven members of a PCA mission church were tried and censured by a provisional Session for opposing the organizing minister's continuation as permanent pastor. The SJC sustained the appeal and reversed the convictions, holding that the indictments lacked required specificity under BCO 32-5, that the accused had no sinned against the fifth or ninth commandment by expressing opinions about ministerial suitability, and that the right to choose a pastor belongs to the congregation—not the provisional Session. A concurring opinion addressed indictment requirements, standard of review in appeals, and congregational rights in mission church minister selection.
